info

scanning)

Ziqi: image processing, cell phenotyping, spatial analysis

Lina: image QC

Matias, Olavi: image analysis for ROIs selection

...: WES and bulk RNA-seq analysis

# 2024.10.02

Meeting: Anniina, Ziqi, Saun, Ada, Matias, Olavi

This will be the first regular SPACE project meeting!

## Introduction of SPACE project

![](media/image57.png){width="6.55553258967629in"
height="3.6767049431321084in"}

chemo-naive samples.

## Discussion regarding DL-driven ROI discovery

Discussion:

- Matias: currently using 3 channels, perform analysis on 22
 > Oncosys-Ova images which is too small sample size. Thus focus on
 > H&E slides. - running with all channels for all WSI images is not
 > possible (?)

s, perform analysis on 22
 > Oncosys-Ova images which is too small sample size. Thus focus on
 > H&E slides. - running with all channels for all WSI images is not
 > possible (?)

 - Matias extracted foundational model features and feed them to a
 > classifier to interpret the potential findings.

- Anniina suggested Matias **write a summary here in the document**
 > for us to better discuss and help.

- Olavi: apply foundational models on H&E slides, found downloading
 > tiff images from allas is painful. Checked the quality of H&E
 > slides. Almost succeed in loading H&Es to the model - currently
 > having an issue with coordinates.

 - The foundational model can provide us ROIs, but if we want ROIs
 > targeting specific goals we might need to train the model.

- Anniina suggested Olavi **change to adjacent H&E slides**, since the
 > SPACE slides is scanned after 5 cycles of tCyCIF and thus get
 > detached or other issues.

- Anniina suggested if the data size is not large enough we can still
 > use the publica dataset (e.g. TCGA..)

Timeline?

- Towards the end of the year we should have a decision on ROI
 > selection and finish the image analysis. At the beginning of next
 > year we should start to get ST data.

Next Steps

- Olavi can report the progress from the foundation model.

- We should move to the in house adjacent slides.

- Ziqi: Finish cell phenotyping.

start to get ST data.

**Matias experiments summary:**

With NKI TMAs, I trained a PDS vs IDS classifier. I got a good
performance using DNA1, CK7 and Vimenting channels as the input to a
multiple instance learning (MIL) model. I also tested using more than
three channels, and the performance was worse than with the previously
mentioned 3 channels (I tried adding more channels to that combo and
tried different ones, but the results were always worse). In the
architecture of that model, I tested using a model trained from scratch
or using pretrained embeddings from UNI and Gigapath as input. UNI
performed the best, although both gigapath and model from scratch
didn\'t have that much of a difference in performance (0.82 vs 0.80 F1
score in the test set). For testing more than 3 channels, I tried using
the model trained from scratch and I also tested getting pre-trained
embeddings (UNI and Gigapath) for different three channels combinations
and then feeding that way more than 3 channels to the MIL model. Still,
the results were worse than with just passing three channels (we should
also consider the limited dataset size for assessing these results).

Currently, I am trying to run models on the tiles (I generated the tiles

st passing three channels (we should
also consider the limited dataset size for assessing these results).

Currently, I am trying to run models on the tiles (I generated the tiles
detecting tissue and removing background) of WSI from NKI images (22
images), with a different task, which is to predict long and short
survival. I tried running model from scratch, and also using UNI
preprocessed embedding for tiles. I had several issues in terms of
memory to load the data for all tiles, and time to process the model
exceeds the time I am getting for a CSC job in Mahti. I need to rethink
the strategy.

 > been enough time to accumulate the data.~~

~

 - ~~Since some patients are new to the cohort, be careful with
 > those \<300 days of PFI_noProgression, as there might not have
 > been enough time to accumulate the data.~~

 - Final threshold: use 365 days to define long/short PFS, and
 > remove patients with \<365 days of PFI_noProgression, since
 > they are not tracked enough time to accumulate the data

# 2025.01.08

Meeting: Anniina, Ziqi, Matias, Olavi, Sara

Goal:

> \(1\) Check ROIs generated by the deep learning method
>
> \(2\) Briefly plan for the WES and bulk RNA-seq analysis (in Feb)
>
> \(3\) Set a date for the next meeting between Jan 20th - 23rd.

Notes:

The raw SPACE H&E images were cropped to keep only the tissue regions.

Matias had some problems with the image format (mrtx?), but the problem
has been solved.

Matias is using multiple insense model; -\> Olavi drew a workflow for
the pipeline during the meeting.

![](media/image37.png){width="6.5in" height="5.308426290463692in"}

Pablo has the script to overlap tCyCIF and H&E slide, we can discuss

**Regarding deep-learning-driven ROI selection**

Size of TMA core: 1mm

**Conclude for today's meeting**

**Regarding deep-learning-driven ROI selection**

The current workflow is using 1 foundational model to generate feature
vectors from H&E datasets (TCGA and SPACE) -\> Design downstream task
(classification, short/long PFS) -\> If the downstream task is
performing well (high accuracy) -\> it is convincing that the feature
vectors (and the attention map) can be used to [represent clinical
information]{.underline} and perform ROIs selection.

**Problem**: if the downstream task is still not performing well after
training on TCGA, the ROIs are not convincing to be used for spatial
transcriptomic sequencing. Note that the dataset we have (including both
TCGA and SPACE) might be not large enough for training.

Olavi suggested plan B: Use multiple foundational models to generate
feature vectors and visualize attention maps -\> choose overlapping high
attention regions (high confidence) as ROIs. No downstream task in this
case.

**Problem**: We need to find a way to [prove that high-confidence ROIs
also have clinical meanings]{.underline}. TBD.

**WES and Bulk RNAseq analysis in Feb**

No time to discuss.
